reason: scripts/validate_active_context_state.py parses '## Próxima fase' in ROADMAP_CANONICAL.md as the live Próxima fase consumed by _check_next_phase_in_transition_table and related cross-field checks against ACTIVE_CONTEXT_STATE.json (current_phase_id=INF-FULL-07, active_next_phase=PURG-01). Adding new phase_id/phase_class rows directly into that table risks breaking validator parsing or cross-field consistency checks for the live PURG-01 route. The macro 'Camadas e Objetivos' transitions are therefore recorded here as a candidate extension, with Lapidarium absorbing Polimento and the new Diagnóstico de Automação / Camada de Construção / Cinzel-mínimo split kept documentary-only.

candidate_macro_transitions.minimum_deliverable: Infernus FULL evidence bundle + vulnerability register + handoff graph + Minos verdict
PURG-EXIT evidence with no unresolved residuals
revalidation verdict per finding (finding_closed/regressed/partially_mitigated/still_open/new_regression_found)
structural gap identified and construction scope carved from the diagnosis
no structural gap blocking BenchUIX/Cinzel
gap-specific automation capability delivered and ready for BenchUIX
benchmark comparison report with evidence vs real alternatives
one end-to-end workflow with 3-5 real executions and no fault injection
consolidation artifact showing internal contradictions removed
EXT-SEC-00 source ledger primario artifact
EXT-SEC-01 threat model artifact
EXT-SEC-02 hardening artifact
EXT-SEC-03 fixtures sinteticas artifact
EXT-SEC-04 closeout artifact, all EXT-SEC 00-04 artifacts present
Cinzel metrics for >=5 simulated SMB scenarios (time, cost, approval, rollback, retry, artifacts)
authorized DAST/pentest report with scope, window, test accounts, synthetic data, rollback, legal authorization
EXT-SEC-06 retest report
explicit Bedrock decision artifact (PASS/WARN/BLOCK/NEEDS_REVIEW/REGRESSION/OBSOLETE)
design partner onboarding + initial feedback cycle artifact
vulnerability management / retest / adversarial emulation cycle artifact
continuous security operations cycle, no terminal next phase

phase_id_mapping.note: Maps macro chain labels onto existing/likely active-context phase_id values to avoid future naming collisions. Preserves existing names; this table does not rename or alter any existing phase_id.
phase_id_mapping.INF-FULL: INF-FULL-01..INF-FULL-07 (already present in live Próxima fase)
phase_id_mapping.PURG-FULL: PURG-PRE, PURG-00, PURG-01..PURG-05, PURG-Sx, PURG-RES, PURG-EXIT (PURG-PRE/PURG-00/PURG-01 already present in live Próxima fase; PURG-02..PURG-EXIT not yet defined)
phase_id_mapping.INF-REVALIDATION: not yet defined in live Próxima fase; candidate id INF-REVALIDATION
phase_id_mapping.DIAGNOSTICO-AUTOMACAO: not yet defined; candidate id DIAGNOSTICO-AUTOMACAO
phase_id_mapping.CAMADA-CONSTRUCAO-AUTOMACAO: not yet defined; candidate id CAMADA-CONSTRUCAO-AUTOMACAO
phase_id_mapping.BENCHUIX: not yet defined; candidate id BENCHUIX (distinct from existing placeholder row 'BENCH-01' which targets CRISOL-01 directly)
phase_id_mapping.CINZEL-MINIMO: not yet defined; candidate id CINZEL-MINIMO
phase_id_mapping.CRISOL: existing live Próxima fase already has a placeholder row 'CRISOL-01 -> BEDROCK-01'; candidate id CRISOL maps conceptually to CRISOL-01
phase_id_mapping.EXT-SEC-00..EXT-SEC-04: not yet defined; candidate ids EXT-SEC-00..EXT-SEC-04
phase_id_mapping.CINZEL-COMPLETO: not yet defined; candidate id CINZEL-COMPLETO
phase_id_mapping.EXT-SEC-05..EXT-SEC-06: not yet defined; candidate ids EXT-SEC-05..EXT-SEC-06
phase_id_mapping.BEDROCK: existing live Próxima fase already has a placeholder row 'BEDROCK-01 -> null (product)'; candidate id BEDROCK maps conceptually to BEDROCK-01
phase_id_mapping.PRODUCT-P2-DESIGN-PARTNER: not yet defined; candidate id PRODUCT-P2-DESIGN-PARTNER, refines the existing 'product' next_phase_class of BEDROCK-01
phase_id_mapping.EXT-SEC-07..EXT-SEC-08-CONTINUOUS: not yet defined; candidate ids EXT-SEC-07, EXT-SEC-08-CONTINUOUS

activation_requirements: explicit operator gate per new phase
schema/validator support for new phase_id/phase_class values
admission decision artifact analogous to PURG-PRE/PURG-00/PURG-01 pattern
no live-route mutation without operator authorization
